applied_optimizations.name: Hybrid PyTorch+Triton approach
applied_optimizations.description: Use PyTorch for efficient reduction (mean/var computation across N,H,W dimensions) and Triton for elementwise batch norm apply. Key optimizations: flat indexing to minimize index calculations, precompute H*W outside loop, 256 programs with BLOCK_SIZE=2048 for good parallelism on MI355X.

attempted_but_failed.name: Pure Triton with loop-based reduction
attempted_but_failed.reason: Loop-based reduction pattern doesn't compile efficiently on ROCm Triton, resulted in 5x slowdown
attempted_but_failed.error: Compilation errors with nested loops and tensor operations

attempted_but_failed.name: Pure Triton with atomic reduction
attempted_but_failed.reason: Multiple kernel passes with atomic operations were slower than PyTorch reference

attempted_but_failed.name: 2D blocking with BLOCK_N x BLOCK_HW
attempted_but_failed.reason: Shape compatibility issues with Triton tensor operations

notes: BatchNorm requires computing mean/var across N*H*W elements per channel - PyTorch's reduction is highly optimized
Pure Triton approach with loops is inefficient on ROCm due to compilation limitations
Hybrid approach (PyTorch reduction + Triton elementwise) achieves best performance
Flat indexing minimizes index calculation overhead in Triton kernel
Achieved 1.22x speedup (4.19ms vs 5.11ms reference) with SCORE: 62.2

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all produced zero output — the agent keeps attempting full rewrites that fail silently. One more attempt with extremely prescriptive, incremental instructions.

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 produced no output, continuing the pattern of trials 2-4. The agent keeps breaking the working solution with full rewrites. One more attempt with absolute minimal, copy-paste-ready changes.

global_notes: Key insight: For reduction-heavy operations like BatchNorm, using PyTorch's optimized reduction + Triton for elementwise can outperform pure Triton on ROCm
Flat indexing with precomputed constants reduces kernel overhead significantly
